source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple sessions in same cwd
notes: Two concurrent pi REPL sessions launched in the SAME cwd via the interactive driver's start_session step (gained after the driver was extended). Each pi process mints its OWN session UUID and writes a distinct transcript under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-slug>/<ts>_<uuid>.jsonl (session_id_assignment:false), so the two distinct session_ids are asserted via new_session/same_session_as relations, not literal ids. LAZY-MATERIALIZATION SCOPE NOTE (discovered live in pi/checkpoint-rewind, applied here): pi creates the per-session JSONL file only AFTER the first user message commits, with turn 1 batched into that initial write — so turn 1's working→ready arc is NOT daemon-observable for either session. The recipe therefore sends TWO prompts per session before start_session/re-drive — turn 1 (lazy materialization, unobservable as an arc but emits transcript_new ⇒ session_birth in ready) and turn 2 (the first observable working→ready arc that binds the session identity for later same_session_as pins). Anchors: s1_birth and s2_birth on ready (after their transcripts appear); the working-anchored phases (s1_turn2_*, s2_turn2_*, s1_turn3_*) prove the independent arcs and stable s1 binding while s2 is alive. PID-BINDING CAVEAT: pi's DiscoverPID = DiscoverPIDByCWD (cwd-only narrowing + a highest-PID disambiguate fallback) — UNLIKE codex's per-rollout-file-writer binding and claudecode's metadata-first lookup; two pi processes sharing one cwd both resolve to the same candidate set, so the daemon CANNOT cleanly bind each same-cwd session to its OWN PID by cwd alone. This spec deliberately does NOT assert 'stable per-PID binding' — that's a known daemon weakness for pi (see assessment.json caveats); it DOES assert what the daemon CAN observe correctly: two distinct session_id rows, each progressing through its own session_birth → working → ready arc on its own UUID transcript, with session 1 staying observable as a distinct id when re-driven after session 2 is alive.

text: Session 1's per-session JSONL transcript appears (after the first user message lazy-materializes it) → a session_id row exists in ready

Session 1's SECOND prompt drives ready → working — the first daemon-observable arc on session 1 (turn 1 is batched into the lazy-materialization write and emits no working transition); binds session 1's transcript id for later same_session_as pins
Session 2 appears as a DISTINCT transcript id in the SAME cwd after start_session + its first prompt lazy-materializes its JSONL — new_session proves it is not session 1
Session 2's SECOND prompt drives ready → working — the first daemon-observable arc on session 2 (turn 1 is batched into its lazy-materialization write); independent of session 1

Session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is still alive — a working transition on session 1's ORIGINAL transcript id proves the row stayed observable as a distinct id (independent of session 2's identity)
